title: × Elyleymus colvillensis
uses: × Elyleymus colvillensis, also known as Colville's wildrye, is a species of grass that has been used in traditional medicine. The plant's roots and leaves are said to have anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful for treating various ailments. It is also believed to have antiseptic and antibacterial properties, which can help to speed up the healing process and prevent infection. Additionally, the plant has been used to treat fever, diarrhea, and other gastrointestinal issues. However, more research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of × Elyleymus colvillensis.
taxonomy: × Elyleymus colvillensis is classified as a hybrid between Elymus caninus and Leymus arenarius. It belongs to the family Poaceae. This classification places it within the order Poales. × Elyleymus colvillensis is a hybrid grass species. Its classification reflects its mixed ancestry.
